title: Western Blot
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ip3a","text":"This protocol describes the standard western blot (immunoblot) technique for detecting specific proteins in a complex sample. The procedure involves separating proteins by molecular weight using SDS-PAGE, transferring them to a membrane, and probing with target-specific antibodies to achieve selective detection. The method provides a reliable approach for protein analysis, including quantification of expression levels and verification of molecular weight.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ikxsm","text":"DTT recipe: 0.25 M Tris-HCl pH 6.8, 200mM DTT, 8% SDS, 30% glycerol, 2mg bromophenol blue","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eogdv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f20bg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"wgcfg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"brlbj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tvatw","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"j1g2e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7cdnx","text":"- Ensure ready-to-use BCA standard concentrations are prepared: 2000, 1500, 750, 500, 250, 125 and 0 µg/µL. add 5 µL of standards (no PBS) in triplicate in set wells. Dilute samples at 1:5 (4 µL PBS : 1 µL sample) in triplicate when needed, then add 100 µL of BCA kit buffers mixed at 1:50 ratio.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ypvsd","text":"- For sample prep: target 20 µg protein in 10 µL sample; ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"s4ktg","text":"- Prepare gels, running buffer, transfer buffer, nitrocellulose membranes, and transfer sandwich components in advance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qwypc","text":"- Prepare blocking solution (5% milk) and TBST; pre-chill primary antibody mix if incubating overnight at 4°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Store in a conical tube in the freezer. Can be used up to 3–4 times before Ab starts to wear off.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":29,"length":110}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"w8xyt","text":"- During last wash before secondary incubation: prepare secondary antibody and let it mix on shaker until ready.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

(cassettes)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"z9bie","text":"- Running buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"p481w","text":"- Transfer buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dfd2j","text":"- Nitrocellulose fixation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85bfo","text":"- 0.4% PFA in PBS (for fixation)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ounpl","text":"- TBST (Tris-buffered saline with Tween)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9h1qn","text":"- 5% milk (blocking solution)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jrre2","text":"- Primary antibodies: BD anti-asyn (Cat# 610787; mouse) at 1:1000; GAPDH (Cat# 14C10; CS rabbit) at 1:10,000","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hefoc","text":"- Secondary antibodies: anti-mouse HRP and anti-rabbit HRP, each used at 1:2000 in 5% milk","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"t1w2h","text":"- ECL solution (prepare 1:1 mixture; ~2 mL per blot)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xhxd2","text":"- Chemidoc (or equivalent imaging system)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qtapi","text":"- Kimwipes or absorbent paper","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qxwnh","text":"- Sheet protector (to place membranes on before applying ECL)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prep samples for BCA assay: add in 5 µL of standards, no PBS. Ready-to-use concentrations should already be made up (2000, 1500, 750, 500, 250, 125 and 0 µg/µL). dil ute samples at 1:5 (so 4 µL PBS : 1 µL sample), then add 100 µL of the kit buffers mixed at a 1:50 ratio.
Boil samples at 95°C for 10 minutes in a dryboil plate listen out for popping of caps, do a quick spindown afterwards .
Load samples (15% acrylamide gels) and run gel in running buffer put gels in cassettes facing inwards. Make note of which samples align with which well in the gels
Run at 80 V until samples leave the stacking layer, then run at 100 V until samples are at the bottom of the gel. Always check to make sure the buffer is bubbling/current is flowing .
Transfer samples in transfer buffer onto a nitrocellulose membrane at 100 V for 1 h.
Assemble transfer sandwich in the following order (from bottom to top): Black side of comb on bottom 2 sponges 1 paper filter Gel Nitrocellulose membrane 1 paper filter 2 sponges
Roll out any bubbles! do not ever touch filter directly, use tweezers for transfers .
When transfer is done, isolate membrane.
Rinse blots in PBS, fix in 0.4% PFA for 30 minutes, then wash with TBST (3 quick rinses).
Block membranes in 5% milk for 60 minutes at room temperature on a shaker.
Incubate membranes in primary antibody overnight at 4°C in 5% milk. can save Ab! Store in a conical tube in the freezer. Can be used up to 3-4 times before Ab starts to wear off.
Wash blot in TBST 3 x 5 minutes. during last wash, make 2º Ab and let mix on shaker until ready.
Incubate in secondary antibody for 1 hour at room temperature with shaking.
Wash blots 3 x 5 minutes in TBST.
Prepare ECL solution (1:1 mixture, ~2 mL per blot) and image on the Chemidoc.

Place membranes on a sheet protector and apply ECL solution ALL over the blot. Incubate for 2 min in ECL and make sure there are no bubbles. Make a pile of Kimwipes and lay them on top of blots to absorb ECL before taking pictures on the Chemidoc.
make samples: 20 µg protein in 10 µL volume with 1XDTT for denaturing.
creator: Jhodi Webster
